units.english_guidance: The fragment says that there are a few/some people; 몇 분 is the honorific counter for people here, not a duration in minutes. Preserve that the thought continues.

facts: 몇 분 counts people honorifically in a clause about people being present.
The utterance is an incomplete lead-in to following context.

units.english_guidance: A short affirmative or conversational acknowledgment such as yes/yeah; do not expand it into a substantive claim.

facts: The unit is a brief affirmative or acknowledgment.

units.korean_gold: 어떤 분은 태국 갔다 오셨다고 했거든요. 근데 태국이 진짜 좋았다고 그러시더라구요.
units.english_guidance: Someone had said they went to or had been to Thailand, and that person reported that Thailand was really good. Natural English may combine the two reported-speech clauses.

facts: An unspecified person is reported to have visited Thailand.
That person is reported to have found Thailand really good.

units.english_guidance: A short affirmative or acknowledgment such as yes/yeah.

facts: The unit is a brief affirmative or acknowledgment.

units.english_guidance: The speaker asks why twice in quick succession; preserve the repetition or its pragmatic force rather than reducing it to an unrelated question.

facts: The speaker asks why.
The why-question is repeated.

units.korean_gold: 아니 그 예전에 코로나 사태 이전에 누나랑
units.english_guidance: An incomplete turn begins with a discourse-level no/well and locates the story before the COVID pandemic, continuing with 누나랑 ('with nuna/an older sister'). Later audio context must determine the relationship; do not complete the clause here.

facts: The story is placed before the COVID pandemic.
The phrase remains incomplete at this unit boundary.
catastrophic_if: The rendering reverses the timeframe and places the story after the pandemic.

facts: The unit includes 누나랑, introducing a woman addressed or described as nuna into the continuing phrase.
catastrophic_if: The rendering assigns a family relationship that contradicts the relationship established by the adjoining audio.

units.english_guidance: Dry-run transcription lead only: the primary transcript marks a short acknowledgment, while the second recognizer heard carry-over from 누나랑. A real drafter must resolve this 0.2-second window from audio.

facts: The primary transcript treats the unit as a brief acknowledgment; this fixture does not resolve the recognizer conflict.

units.english_guidance: A fragment explicitly referring to the speaker's biological older sister; the subject marker shows the phrase continues.

facts: 친누나 explicitly denotes the speaker's biological older sister.
The marked subject fragment continues into later speech.
catastrophic_if: The rendering changes the biological older sister into a different person or family relationship.

units.english_guidance: Dry-run transcription lead only: the primary transcript marks an acknowledgment, while the second recognizer heard 친누나가. A real drafter must resolve this boundary from audio.

facts: The primary transcript treats the unit as a brief acknowledgment; this fixture does not resolve the recognizer conflict.

units.korean_gold: 그 막 그 매형이랑 태국에 한 달 살기를 하려고 했는데
units.english_guidance: The continuing story involves the speaker's brother-in-law and a plan to spend/live one month in Thailand; the clause ends with a contrastive 'but' and continues. Avoid forcing an English subject not established inside this window.

facts: 매형 identifies the speaker's older sister's husband/brother-in-law.
The plan involves spending one month in Thailand.
The clause is contrastive and unfinished at the boundary.
catastrophic_if: The rendering changes the brother-in-law into a different family relationship or unrelated participant.

units.korean_gold: 그 내용이 너무 바쁘셔가지고 남는 사람이
units.english_guidance: Dry-run transcription lead only: the primary transcript has 내용이, while the corroborating recognizer heard 매형이. The clause says someone or something was very busy and continues toward 'the person left/available'; a real drafter must resolve the noun from audio.

facts: The clause gives being very busy as the reason and continues into identifying the person left or available.
This fixture does not resolve the 내용이 versus 매형이 recognizer conflict.

units.english_guidance: Dry-run transcription lead only: the primary transcript marks a short acknowledgment, while the second recognizer heard 사람이. A real drafter must resolve this boundary from audio.

facts: The primary transcript treats the unit as a brief acknowledgment; this fixture does not resolve the recognizer conflict.

units.korean_gold: 저밖에 없어서 그 공짜로 태국 한 달 살기를 한 적이 있어요 아 근데 그 치앙마이에서 한 달을 살았는데 너무 동네가 예쁘고 사람들도 너무 친절하고 한국어
units.english_guidance: The speaker was the only person available and therefore once stayed/lived in Thailand for a month for free. They lived in Chiang Mai for a month, found the neighborhood very pretty and the people very kind, then the clip cuts off after beginning 'Korean...'. Preserve the repeated one-month duration and the unfinished ending.

facts: The speaker was the only person available.
The speaker once spent one month in Thailand for free.
catastrophic_if: The rendering changes who went to Thailand or reverses the fact that the stay was free.

facts: The speaker lived in Chiang Mai for one month.
The neighborhood was very pretty and the people were very kind.
The recorded window cuts off after the word Korean begins the next thought.
